sglang.title: Add cuda event based on waiting value
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
For the scenarios where the record happens later than the wait.
stream_a = torch.cuda.Stream()
stream_b = torch.cuda.Stream()

with torch.cuda.stream(stream_a):
    event.wait()

with torch.cuda.stream(stream_b):
    event.record()

sglang.title: Fix NSA Bug in Centralize NSA Dispatch Logic
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Fix operator precedence bug in set_nsa_prefill_impl that caused use_mha to always be True on H200 (SM90), bypassing the max_kv_len <= nsa_index_topk check.

When req.is_retracted == True, in order to preserve the existing output_ids without clearing them, we set fill_ids = origin_input_ids + output_ids, which includes output_ids. Consequently, extend_input_len = len(fill_ids) - len(prefix_indices) also incorporates the le

vllm.title: [crashfix] Eagle + multimodal can crash on mm cache miss
vllm.author: mickaelseznec
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Eagle shifts tokens by one when doing its forward, but main doesn't. Meaning that eagle might need to access some mm data at the boundary, which hasn't been encoded yet in the case of a partial prefill.
Fix the base model scheduler by rolling back to mm_start - 1 - eagle_shift to avoid eagle

Signed-off-by: Alexei V. Ivanov alexei.ivanov@amd.com

vllm.title: [BugFix] Preserve spec decoding uniform decode when scheduling
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
@benchislett had reported that removing the optimization in #27922 significantly improves async eagle spec decoding performance.
I had assumed this was due to requests missing steps and having to be re-scheduled for the last one or two tokens and opened #29542 to address this, but didn't get a chanc

vllm.title: SM120 / NVFP4: add device guard and runtime SM dispatch to cutlass_scaled_fp4_mm
vllm.author: hholtmann
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
fix(cuda): add device guard and runtime SM dispatch to cutlass_scaled_fp4_mm
Purpose
Currently, the fp4 scaled_mm function doesn't work for the 5090 GPU, resulting in a RuntimeError: Internal Error. See #21274 and #22783 for more informatio
Test Plan
pytest -v -s tests/kernels/quantization/test_nvfp

vllm.title: [ROCm] Fallback pytorch GELU with tanh approximation to GELU()
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There is a bug when using native Pytorch's implementation of F.gelu(..., approximate='tanh')  with torch.compile on ROCm. Because of this, models such as gemma3 and starcoder2 produces garbage output.
This PR is a quick fix "band-aid" solution to avoid using tanh approximation and fallback to the ge

vllm.title: [responsesAPI][4] fix responseOutputItem Kimi K2 thinking bug
vllm.author: qandrew
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Kimi K2 thinking occasionally outputs a output item before MCP call. This PR is a minor fix but a prerequisite for #29798, which implements the full MCP loop
fix a bug where we assume the item type is dict when it might not be.

Test Plan
Unit tests pass; spun up k2 thinking and this doesn'

vllm.title: [Core] Support resetting all running requests' KV while calling `reset_prefix_cache`
vllm.author: zhuohan123
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Currently, the reset_prefix_cache call can actually fail: When there are still running requests and kv cache with ref count > 0 in the system, the call will refuse to reset the prefix cache. This PR adds an option to move running requests back to waiting queue and ensure the reset call can b

vllm.title: [Misc] Throw error on unintended access to scheduler_config.max_model_len
vllm.author: frank-wei
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
There is a risk when people unintentionally try to access scheduler_config.max_model_len and it is returned as 8192. SInce it is removed in #28733, we should give people AttributeError as alert.
Test Plan
Test Result
Before the fix:



from vllm.config import VllmConfig
vllm_config = VllmCon

vllm.title: [Misc] Add ReplicaId to Ray metrics
vllm.author: eicherseiji
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Taking over #22159 by @lengrongfu
Currently, Ray metrics only include WorkerId in default tags. ReplicaId is the more readable, dashboard-visible way to distinguish model replicas in metrics.

Test Plan
Run Ray Serve LLM app:
from ray import serve
from ray.serve.llm import LLMConfig, build_

vllm.title: Add missing return in _check_vllm_model_embed_input_ids
vllm.author: jcyang43
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add missing return in _check_vllm_model_embed_input_ids. Without the missing return statement, the method will return False on the first time but True on subsequent times. Adds the missing return to ensure consistency and avoid confusion.
Thanks @richjames0 for identifying the issue & the fi

vllm.title: [Misc] Update conftest for entrypoints/sagemaker test folder
vllm.author: zhaozuy
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Update SageMaker test server to explicitly enable sagemaker stateful sessions management.
Test Plan
Rerun relevant SageMaker tests.
pytest tests/entrypoints/openai/sagemaker/test_sagemaker_stateful_sessions.py
Test Result
pytest tests/entrypoints/sagemaker/test_sagemaker_stateful_sessions.py

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix index error in ngram_proposer
vllm.author: usberkeley
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
There is an index variable usage error in the batch_propose_numba function. The code incorrectly uses the loop index i to write to the valid_ngram_num_drafts and valid_ngram_draft arrays, when it should use the actual request index idx.

Incorrect behavior: When valid_ngram_requests contains

vllm.title: [CI][ROCm] Fix test_correctness_sliding_window
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
GELU(approximate="tanh") is unstable with torch.compile on ROCm
pytest -s -v test_correctness_sliding_window.py

The models in this test - gemma-3-1b-it and starcoder2-3b use GELU with tanh approximation. The pytorch's native implemention of F.gelu(..., approximate='tanh') used during torch.compile

vllm.title: [CI][AMD] spec_decode:eagle skip FLASH_ATTN for deepseek on ROCm
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR skips FLASH_ATTN for deepseek model as it is not supported on ROCm.
[CI][ROCM] pytest:
pytest -s -v test_spec_decode.py::test_eagle_correctness -k deepseek_eagle

vllm.title: [Chore] Move tokenizer initialization methods
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer.get_tokenizer-> vllm.tokenizers.get_tokenizer (BC until v0.13)
vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer.cached_get_tokenizer-> vllm.tokenizers.cached_get_tokenizer (BC until v0.13)
vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer.cached_tokenizer_from_config -> vllm.tokenizers.c

vllm.title: [CI] Renovation of nightly wheel build & generation (take 2)
vllm.author: Harry-Chen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a rework of #29690 (and later bug fixes #29787, #29792). They were reverted or closed due to bugs in setup.py that led to unsuccessful installation.
Purpose
vllm's nightly build needs to be renovated due to the following reasons:

Links to aarch64 wheels are totally broken.
Binary wheels are

vllm.title: [CPU]Parallelize over tokens in int4 moe
vllm.author: xiangze-arm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This patch changes the parallelization strategy from over experts to over tokens.
When experts have very different numbers of tokens, the old approach could lead to thread imbalance. By iterating over total tokens instead of experts, the workload can be distributed more evenly.
Test Plan
Tes

vllm.title: Fix some Transformers nightly tests
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration, Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration, JinaVLForRanking - Uses Qwen 2/2.5 VL which recently had the hacky __getattribute__ removed in huggingface/transformers#42420. This means that you cannot just get config fields from the root config and expect them to get fetched fr

vllm.title: [Core] Eliminate redundant is_encoder_decoder lookups (>20us/token)
vllm.author: wushidonguc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Cache model_config.is_encoder_decoder once at the start of _preprocess() instead of accessing it multiple times within the method to eliminate redundant property lookups.
Impact: Saves >20 microseconds per inference step by eliminating redundant property lookups in the hot path.
Performance

vllm.title: [BugFix] respect VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL in logger
vllm.author: BoyuanFeng
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Issue: vLLM defaults logger vllm to be DEBUG (Line 65). envs.VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL is used to update DEFAULT_LOGGING_CONFIG["handlers"]["vllm"] (aka logging.StreamHandler), but not DEFAULT_LOGGING_CONFIG["loggers"]["vllm"] (aka logging.vllm). logging.vllm is DEBUG and used as the parent of all other lo
